conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand a bit more about Alex's situation since he's 18 years old.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training? For example, is he enrolled in full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent) or an approved training program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in education - he's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is studying for his A-levels, I need to confirm a few more details.

When did Alex turn 18, or when will he turn 18? (I need to know the specific date if possible, or at least which month and year)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm sorry, I don't have his exact birth date to hand at the moment. Is that something you absolutely need right now, or can we work with the fact that he's 18 and currently doing his A-levels?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine! We can work with the information that he's 18 and currently doing his A-levels. Let me ask a different question that will help me assess his eligibility.

Has Alex left his A-level course, or is he still actively enrolled and attending? If he's still enrolled, do you know approximately when his course is due to end?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's still actively enrolled and attending. His A-levels won't finish until next summer when he takes his final exams - so that'll be around June or July next year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you, that's helpful. I have a few more questions to determine eligibility.

First, does Alex live with you, or does he live elsewhere (for example, in student accommodation or with someone else)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your circumstances:

Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you have parental responsibility for him and are you the person who is primarily responsible for his care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm his parent and I'm responsible for him. I look after him and he lives here with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions:

Do you currently live in the UK, and are you a UK resident (or an EU/EEA/Swiss national with a right to reside in the UK)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. One final question before I can give you my assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else claimed it recently?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Before I give you my final assessment, let me review the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information about Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official Child Benefit rules, I can now give you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Even though Alex is 18, you still qualify for Child Benefit because:
- He is under 20 years old
- He is in approved education (his A-level studies at sixth form college count as approved education)
- He lives with you
- You are responsible for him as his parent
- You live in the UK
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You should be able to continue claiming Child Benefit for Alex as long as he remains enrolled in his A-level course. This would typically continue until the end of his education or when he turns 20, whichever comes first.

**Important note:** If you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit itself.

If you need to apply or have any questions about your claim, you can contact the Child Benefit Office directly.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. According to the official rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, BUT you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex is 18 years old and is currently actively enrolled in and attending A-level studies at sixth form college, which counts as approved education. He lives with you at home, you are his parent with responsibility for him, you live in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Since he is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels), you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he qualifies under the extended eligibility rules because he is under 20 and remains in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). The claimant is responsible for Alex, he lives with them in the UK, and no one else is claiming for him.

He lives with you at home, you are his parent with responsibility for him, you live in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Since he is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels), you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he qualifies under the extended eligibility rules because he is under 20 and remains in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). The claimant is responsible for Alex, he lives with them in the UK, and no one else is claiming for him.
